Program_Description: Presumptive eligibility (PE) is early, temporary Medi-Cal for low income pregnant women, providing basic prenatal care while waiting for Medi-Cal approval.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must apply for Medi-Cal and be living in California
Application_Process: Apply for Medi-Cal while on PE
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: Medi-Cal Access Program (MCAP) is low-cost health care coverage for pregnant women.
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: AIM is for middle-income families who don’t have health insurance and whose income is too high for no-cost Medi-Cal.
Eligibility_Requirements: Income too high for no-cost Medi-Cal
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: The Healthy Families Program provides health coverage for newborns of women enrolled in AIM.
Target_Population: Newborns of women enrolled in AIM
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: The California Home Visiting Program (CHVP) is designed for overburdened families, providing tools and know-how to independently raise their children.
Target_Population: Overburdened families, parents during pregnancy and in the first few years of life
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: CPSP is a Medi-Cal program that provides enhanced obstetric services for eligible low-income, pregnant, and postpartum women.
Eligibility_Requirements: Eligible low-income pregnant and postpartum women
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: The Sudden Infant Death Syndrome Program provides information and resources to reduce the risk of SIDS in infants.
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.

Program_Description: WIC serves: All foster children under five years old who meet eligibility requirements; Breastfeeding women up to one year postpartum; All documented/undocumented persons in a family who meet eligibility requirements.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children under five years old.
Eligibility_Requirements: To qualify, you must meet the income guidelines or the person for whom you are applying must have active Medi-Cal, food stamps, or TANF.
Notes: No direct match to maternal health programs in the registry.
